zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert FitzGerold於1067年8月之前出生在英國波克夏金斯頓 李爾時，他的父親Seigneur Giroldus FitzGerold de la Ville Tancréde - Seigneur Gérold de Roumara - le Dapifero de Normandie et Châtelain de Neufmarché11064歲，母親Albreda de Normandie Lady de Roumare11064歲。他和Alice  Albereda Le Meschin至少有7個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1141年12月之後在英國英格蘭波克夏。

nameMeanings.name: FitzGerold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Jarrell
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex; of Norman origin): variant of Jarrold, itself a variant of Gerald .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert FitzGerold was born before August 1067, in Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, England, his father, Seigneur Giroldus FitzGerold de la Ville Tancréde - Seigneur Gérold de Roumara - le Dapifero de Normandie et Châtelain de Neufmarché, was 11064 and his mother, Albreda de Normandie Lady de Roumare, was 11064. He had at least 7 sons and 1 daughter with Alice  Albereda Le Meschin. He died after December 1141, in Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert FitzGerold est né avant août 1067, à Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Seigneur Giroldus FitzGerold de la Ville Tancréde - Seigneur Gérold de Roumara - le Dapifero de Normandie et Châtelain de Neufmarché, avait 11064 ans et sa mère, Albreda de Normandie Lady de Roumare, avait 11064 ans. Il a eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille avec Alice  Albereda Le Meschin. Il est décédé après décembre 1141, à Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hatte mit Alice  Albereda Le Meschin mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb nach Dezember 1141 in Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert FitzGeroldが1067年8月以前にイギリスバークシュアイアキングストン リスルで生まれた時，父Seigneur Giroldus FitzGerold de la Ville Tancréde - Seigneur Gérold de Roumara - le Dapifero de Normandie et Châtelain de Neufmarchéは11064歳，母Albreda de Normandie Lady de Roumareは11064歳であった。彼はAlice  Albereda Le Meschinとの間に少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1141年12月以後にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアで死去した。

ko.Boughton Malherb Kent England: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 부그h턴 운더 블리인
ko.lifeSummary: Robert FitzGerold(이)가 1067년 8월 이전 영국 버크실르 킨그스턴 리슬일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Seigneur Giroldus FitzGerold de la Ville Tancréde - Seigneur Gérold de Roumara - le Dapifero de Normandie et Châtelain de Neufmarché(은)는 11064세였고, 그의 어머니 Albreda de Normandie Lady de Roumare(은)는 11064세였다. 그는 Alice  Albereda Le Meschin(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1141년 12월 이후 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert FitzGerold родился перед августа 1067 г. в местности кингстон лисл, биркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Seigneur Giroldus FitzGerold de la Ville Tancréde - Seigneur Gérold de Roumara - le Dapifero de Normandie et Châtelain de Neufmarché было 11064 года, а матери, которую звали Albreda de Normandie Lady de Roumare, 11064. У него было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь от Alice  Albereda Le Meschin. Он умер после декабрь 1141 г. в местности биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert FitzGerold nació antes de agosto de 1067, en Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Seigneur Giroldus FitzGerold de la Ville Tancréde - Seigneur Gérold de Roumara - le Dapifero de Normandie et Châtelain de Neufmarché, tenía 11064 años y su mamá, Albreda de Normandie Lady de Roumare, tenía 11064 años. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija con Alice  Albereda Le Meschin. Murió después de diciembre de 1141, en Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
